title: Tapping for the Unforgivable Person
subtitle: An EFT sequence for working with the resentment you are not ready to release
excerpt: An EFT tapping practice for the person you are not ready to forgive: the one where the harm was real, the accountability never came, and the idea of forgiveness feels like a further violation. Works with the resentment as it is, not as it should be.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you are carrying active resentment toward someone who harmed you and the idea of forgiveness currently feels impossible or wrong.

content.text: A five-minute EFT tapping practice for the person you are not ready to forgive. This script does not ask for forgiveness. It works with the resentment as it is, honours the validity of the anger, and creates a small amount of room in the nervous system without requiring you to arrive anywhere specific.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point and say each statement once:
Even though what this person did was real and harmful and I am not ready to let it go, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though the idea of forgiving them feels like excusing what happened, I accept what I feel about this.
Even though I am carrying this and I'm not sure I want to put it down yet, I am open to a little more ease right now.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: The anger I have been carrying.
Side of Eye: What they did and that they never accounted for it.
Under Eye: The injustice of being the one who has to do this work.
Under Nose: My anger as a record of what happened.
Chin: The cost of carrying this in my body all this time.
Collarbone: The part of me that stays alert for this person.
Under Arm: What would it take for me to be ready to release this?
Top of Head: I am allowed to take my time.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: My anger was a valid response. It did its job.
Side of Eye: I do not have to excuse what they did to ease my own nervous system.
Under Eye: Carrying this is a choice I can revisit.
Under Nose: The resentment belongs to me, not to them. I decide when to set it down.
Chin: I can hold what happened as real and still choose ease.
Collarbone: Being willing to consider release is enough for today.
Under Arm: I release a fraction. That is a beginning.
Top of Head: I am in charge of this. I move at my pace.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Written for homemade.education. The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
